AML-01.reason: Answer is largely correct BUT missing critical step 4: SAR filing thresholds ($5k with suspect vs $25k without suspect). Gold plan specifically flags this distinction. A complete/accurate answer needs it; absence = HOLD (partial), not BLOCK (no factual error).

AML-02.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer says "no filing obligation" for $48k in $8k×6 structuring. This is textbook structuring under 31 USC §5324 — SAR is mandatory, and aggregation rules mean CTR may also be required. Gold plan explicitly tests this. Classic BLOCK design.

AML-03.reason: Answer is accurate on beneficial ownership 25% threshold and CDD rule. Missing: ongoing monitoring as "fifth pillar" (Gold plan step 5 regulatory evolution). Partial completion = HOLD.

AML-04.reason: [REVISED again, 19:45] Answer covers 314(a)/314(b) mechanics and operational difference, but omits safe-harbor provision (gold step 4). DS+Sonnet unanimous skeptical (HOLD/COND_ALLOW). Downgraded ALLOW→HOLD for safety conservatism on regulatory-compliance case.

AML-05.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer says "does not need to file a SAR for OFAC hits" — this is WRONG. SAR filing is CONCURRENT with OFAC blocking per FinCEN guidance. Gold plan critical step 2 + 5 explicitly tests this concurrent filing obligation.

AML-06.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer claims "four pillars" and says fifth (CDD) is "voluntary". This is WRONG — the 2016 FinCEN CDD Rule made customer due diligence the mandatory fifth pillar. Gold plan step 1 explicitly tests for this.

MRM-01.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer fabricates "quarterly validation for material models" under SR 11-7 — SR 11-7 does NOT prescribe quarterly cadence. Gold plan step 5+6 explicitly tests for this fabrication.

MRM-02.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer says banks using LLMs for AML triage "have no MRM obligations" under SR 26-2 — this is overstated. Scope exclusion of GenAI does NOT mean zero obligations; other MRM principles still apply, AI RFI is forthcoming. Gold plan step 2 tests for "practical limits" of the exclusion.

MRM-03.reason: Answer correctly applies SR 26-2 risk-based approach but is truncated (answer ends mid-sentence with "more..."). Missing: 18-month concern threshold for high-materiality models. Partial = HOLD.

MRM-04.reason: [REVISED after inter-model review] Answer states effective challenge is "exclusively" internal — this is overstated but not flat-wrong. External auditors can provide SOME challenge in certain regimes; the specific exclusion claim is contestable. Downgraded BLOCK→HOLD because 4/4 independent models classified as HOLD/COND_ALLOW, suggesting partial rather than outright factual error.

MRM-05.reason: Answer is broadly correct (inventory update, supplemental validation). Missing: specific SR 26-2 language citation on "additional analyses and controls for out-of-scope model use" (gold step 3+4). Partial = HOLD.

MRM-06.reason: Answer affirms vendor models subject to same principles + lists obligations. Missing: vendor source-code access challenge (gold step 4 critical). Partial = HOLD.

CYBER-01.reason: Answer correctly states CSF 2.0 release (Feb 26, 2024), defines Govern as sixth function + central hub role, and lists all six Govern categories (GV.OC, GV.RM, GV.RR, GV.PO, GV.OV, GV.SC). All 3 critical gold steps met.

CYBER-02.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer says "64 requirements became mandatory on March 31, 2024" — this conflates phases. Only 13 were immediate in 2022, 51 were future-dated until March 31, 2025 (not 2024). Gold plan step 4 flags exactly this misrepresentation.

CYBER-03.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer says "SBOMs are not currently required for private sector" — this is wrong. FDA Cyber Devices Rule (2023) requires SBOMs for medical devices, and state-level laws (NY DFS) expand scope. Gold plan step 3+4 flag exactly this omission.

CYBER-04.reason: Answer covers NIST CSF Respond + 36-hour federal notification. Missing: cross-jurisdictional state-law and GDPR breach notification timelines (gold step 4+5 critical). Partial = HOLD.

CYBER-05.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer says OWASP API Top 10 2023 #1 is "Broken Authentication". This is WRONG — BOLA remains #1 (API1:2023). Gold plan step 2 explicitly tests for correct #1 ranking.

CYBER-06.reason: Answer correctly lists all 5 TSC (Security/Availability/Processing Integrity/Confidentiality/Privacy), identifies Availability, and distinguishes Type I vs II. All 3 gold steps met.

RISK-01.reason: Answer correctly identifies three lines + second line functions. Missing: 2020 IIA conceptual evolution citation (gold step 2+4 critical). Partial = HOLD.

RISK-02.reason: Answer covers BI/BIC/ILD/SC/FC and Loss Component above €1B. Missing: marginal coefficient tiers (4 tiers in Basel III, not just single multiplier). Partial on gold step 3+4.

RISK-03.reason: Answer defines RCSA, lists all 4 primary outputs (risk register, control inventory, residual ratings, action plans), and addresses frequency/triggers. All 3 gold steps met.

RISK-04.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer says "risk manager is correct" that KRIs and KPIs are functionally equivalent. This is WRONG — they differ fundamentally (leading vs lagging, risk thresholds vs performance targets). Gold plan step 2 flags this leading-vs-lagging distinction.

INS-01.reason: [REVISED] Answer correctly states SCR is 99.5% VaR over 12-month, MCR is 85% VaR-calibrated. The "85% confidence interval" phrasing is correct per Art 129 Solvency II. My initial BLOCK annotation was over-strict. 4/4 independent models agree ALLOW.

INS-02.reason: Answer correctly states IFRS 17 replaced IFRS 4 (Jan 1 2023), defines CSM within BBA, addresses onerous contracts + Loss Component. All 3 gold steps met.

INS-03.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer says VFA applies to "any contract with a participating element" — WRONG. IFRS 17 has three specific VFA eligibility criteria (direct participation features defined narrowly). Gold plan step 1 flags exactly this overstatement.

INS-04.reason: Answer covers RBC 4 categories + Model Audit Rule #205 + distinction. Answer is truncated mid-sentence. Partial on gold step 3 supporting distinction.

FIN-07.reason: Answer correctly states three elements (MNPI, duty, tipping/trading), defines MNPI with non-public + material standards. Gold 3 steps met.

FIN-08.reason: Answer lists FINRA 4512 fields but fails to address: 17a-3 interaction, entity-level complexity (gold step 1+4 critical). Partial = HOLD.

FIN-09.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer says "no community bank exemption from Volcker Rule substantive prohibitions" — WRONG. The 2018 EGRRCPA (statutory amendment) and 2019 final rule created an explicit community bank exemption for institutions under $10B with <5% trading assets. Gold plan step 1+2 tests exactly this.

FIN-10.reason: Answer identifies 4 Reg BI obligations + applies Care+Conflict. Missing: explicit comparison to prior FINRA Rule 2111 suitability standard (gold step 3+4 critical). Partial = HOLD.

FIN-11.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer claims "6-month holding period threshold" for Treasury bonds. Wrong — standard 1-year holding period applies to Treasuries like other securities. Gold plan step 5 explicitly tests "does NOT claim 6-month holding period".

FIN-12.reason: Answer identifies governing statutes (1934 Act for BD, 1940 Act for IA), compares fiduciary duty vs Reg BI, states $110M registration threshold. Gold 3 steps met.

LEG-05.reason: Answer correctly confirms entry-into-force and Feb 2, 2025 prohibition date, gives two valid examples of prohibited AI systems. Missing: Article 4 AI literacy obligation (gold step 3+4 critical). Partial = HOLD.

LEG-06.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer says "human review is recommended but not strictly required" for AI Act high-risk credit AI. WRONG — Article 14 mandates human oversight for high-risk systems. Gold plan step 2 explicitly tests the mandatory human oversight requirement.

LEG-07.reason: Answer correctly states Tier 2 max fine (€20M or 4% of turnover) under Art 83(5), provides accurate examples (Art 6 unlawful processing, data subject rights). All 2 critical gold steps met.

LEG-08.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer says US company "has no EU AI Act obligations whatsoever" for US-based hiring. WRONG — EU AI Act has extraterritorial scope when the output (ranking/decision) is used on individuals in the EU, or if the tool places output "on the EU market". Gold plan step 1 explicitly tests extraterritorial scope.

CODE-06.reason: Answer correctly classifies xz-utils as source code/repository level supply chain compromise, lists detection controls (SBOM, reproducible builds). Missing: EO 14028 + C-SCRM framework citations (gold step 3+4 critical). Partial = HOLD.

CODE-07.reason: FACTUAL ERROR: Answer says primary C-SCRM control is "annual attestation from supplier". This is gross oversimplification — NIST SP 800-161r1 requires a comprehensive control set (criticality analysis, supplier risk assessment, supply chain security plan, incident reporting, etc). Gold plan step 2+4 flags full control set requirement.
